_comment: All rates are evidence-based guidelines (ISSN / Burke et al.). Adjust as needed.

carb_rates_g_per_hour._comment: Base carb intake targets by race type, indexed by effort tier

_comment: Secondary multiplier applied on top of the effort-indexed base carb rate.
Physiological rationale (ISSN / Burke et al.):
easy      0.85 — at sub-threshold efforts fat oxidation contributes more
(~50-60% VO2max); carbohydrate demand is reduced, so the base
rate is scaled down to avoid over-fuelling and GI distress.
race_pace 1.00 — the base rates in carb_rates_g_per_hour are calibrated
for race-pace effort; no additional adjustment needed.
hard      1.15 — above threshold (~85%+ VO2max) muscles rely almost
exclusively on glycogen. A 15% uplift targets higher turnover
and supports delayed fatigue in shorter, intense efforts.

condition_modifiers._comment: Sodium and fluid uplifts for environmental conditions

sodium_targets_mg_per_hour._comment: Base sodium target before condition modifier. Scales with body weight.

fluid_targets_ml_per_hour._comment: Base fluid target before condition modifier. Scales with body weight.

gender_modifiers._comment: Small downward adjustment for female athletes (lower avg sweat rate)

timing_rules._comment: When to consume each product type relative to race start (minutes)

during.note: First intake at ~20 min, then every 30 min

caffeine_rules.note: Caffeine gels replace one plain gel slot when caffeine_ok = true

training_mode._comment: In training mode reduce carb targets to train the gut at lower doses

_comment: Select which carb calculation strategy to use.
Options: effort_based, duration_based, hybrid, distance_adaptive (default)
distance_adaptive: linearly interpolates between research anchors from goal_minutes.
This ensures every unique race duration produces a unique carb target.
See src/strategies/carb-strategies.js for implementation details.

elevation_modifiers._comment: Carb and sodium uplifts based on average course grade (elevation_gain_m / distance_m * 100). Climbing increases glycogen demand and core temperature.

_comment: Athlete profile modifiers for carb, sodium, and fluid targets.
Accounts for variation in absorption capacity and sweat rate based on training.
Untrained athletes have 30-40% lower sweat rates and carb absorption.
Elite athletes can tolerate higher rates due to training adaptations.

untrained.description: Lower sweat rate, reduced carb absorption capacity

trained.description: Higher sweat rate, greater absorption capacity, better thermoregulation

elite.description: Optimized sweat control, maximum absorption capacity

vo2max_modifiers._comment: For future VO2Max-adjusted strategy (if athlete provides fitness data)

_comment: Future: continuous temperature input (Celsius) instead of categorical conditions.
Maps ambient temperature to sodium/fluid multipliers via curve.
Base: 20°C (mild). Sweat rate ~2-3% increase per °C above baseline.
Validation and warning thresholds for carb, sodium, fluid recommendations.
Helps catch potentially problematic fueling strategies.

carb.warning_high_short_race: For events <45 min, carb intake is typically unnecessary
carb.warning_high_carb_rate: For short races, carb rates >30 g/h increase GI distress risk

sodium.warning_hot_humidity: Consider pre-race sodium loading (20.5g in 2.5L water, 2-4 hrs before start)

fluid.warning_overhydration: Intakes >1000 ml/h increase hyponatremia risk in events <2 hours
